Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
feat: Add LSP + MCP integration for Claude Code orchestration (v1.2.0)

Implement complete Language Server Protocol (LSP) and Model Context Protocol (MCP)
integration to enable AI-driven spec execution via Claude Code.

## Major Features

- Language Server with LSP + MCP support
  - 6 MCP tools for AI agent integration
  - Custom LSP methods for extension communication
  - Auto-creates .vscode/mcp.json for VSCode native MCP support

- Security & Validation
  - Input validation prevents path traversal attacks
  - Sanitizes specId and taskId parameters
  - Status validation for task updates

- Flexible Task Parser
  - Supports both **T001**: and #1 task formats
  - Parses GitHub Spec Kit YAML frontmatter
  - Handles task dependencies and parallel execution

## MCP Tools Implemented

1. specgofer_get_specs - Get all specifications
2. specgofer_get_next_task - Get next available task
3. specgofer_execute_task - Execute a specific task
4. specgofer_update_task_status - Update task status
5. specgofer_validate_code - Validate against constitution (placeholder)
6. specgofer_run_tests - Run tests for a spec (placeholder)

## Critical Fixes

- Fixed task parser regex to support multiple formats
- Fixed MCP config path resolution for VSIX packaging
- Added input validation for security hardening

## Technical Details

- Language Server: TypeScript, vscode-languageserver 9.0.1
- Extension: Webpack bundled, vscode-languageclient 9.0.1
- MCP: VSCode 1.102+ native support via experimental capabilities
- Package: 7.52 MB VSIX with bundled Language Server

## Files Added

- language-server/src/server.ts - Main LSP + MCP server
- language-server/src/mcp/toolHandler.ts - MCP tool implementations
- language-server/src/utils/specKitLoader.ts - Spec parsing logic
- extension/src/lspClient.ts - LSP client wrapper
- extension/src/mcpConfig.ts - MCP configuration helper
- extension/src/specKitParser.ts - GitHub Spec Kit parser

## Files Modified

- extension/src/extension.ts - Integrated LSP client & MCP config
- extension/src/progressProvider.ts - Uses SpecKitParser
- extension/package.json - Version bump to 1.2.0, added dependencies

## Documentation

- TESTING_GUIDE.md - Comprehensive testing procedures
- IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md - Full implementation details
- CRITICAL_FIXES_APPLIED.md - Summary of all fixes
- LATEST_INTEGRATION_RESEARCH.md - VSCode/Claude/Copilot research
- extension/CHANGELOG.md - Version history

## Testing

- Test spec created: .specify/specs/test-001/
- Manual testing protocol documented
- Engineer review completed (score: 82/100)

## Breaking Changes

None. Fully backward compatible with v1.1.0.

## Next Steps

- Manual testing with Claude Code extension
- Add automated unit tests
- Implement constitutional validation
- Add file watching for auto-refresh

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")

Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
fix: Language Server path resolution in packaged VSIX (v1.2.1)

Fix critical bug where Language Server could not be found when running from
installed VSIX package, causing "Language Server not found" error on startup.

## Problem

The LSP client was looking for the Language Server at the wrong path:
- Development: `../language-server/dist/server.js` (parent directory)
- Production VSIX: `language-server/dist/server.js` (inside extension)

The code only checked the development path, causing production installations
to fail with "SpecGofer Language Server not found" error.

## Solution

Added intelligent path resolution with fallback:
1. First try production path: `language-server/dist/server.js`
2. Fallback to development path: `../language-server/dist/server.js`
3. Show helpful error with attempted path if both fail
4. Log successful path resolution for debugging

## Changes

- extension/src/lspClient.ts: Added dual-path resolution logic
- extension/package.json: Version bump to 1.2.1
- extension/CHANGELOG.md: Added v1.2.1 release notes

## Testing

Verified:
- ✅ Works in development (parent directory structure)
- ✅ Works in VSIX (bundled structure)
- ✅ Helpful error messages if Language Server missing
- ✅ Logs resolved path to Output channel

## Files Modified

- extension/src/lspClient.ts
- extension/package.json
- extension/CHANGELOG.md

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)")
